text_en: Patient not breathing, lips blue, no pulse. Bystander CPR in progress.
Child 3 years. Active seizure ongoing for 8 minutes. Unresponsive. Eyes rolled back.
Adult male. Uncontrolled bleeding from femoral wound. RR 32, HR 148, SBP 72, AVPU P.
Woman 28 weeks pregnant. Seized 2 minutes ago. Still convulsing. BP 180/120.
Adult female. Severe allergic reaction after eating shellfish. Throat swelling, stridor audible. SpO2 82%, HR 138, SBP 74. Unresponsive to voice.
note: Anaphylactic shock — SpO2 < 85 and SBP < 80 with HR > 130

text_en: Child 2 years. Active generalised seizure lasting 12 minutes. Unresponsive between episodes. High fever 40.1°C.
note: Status epilepticus with fever — emergency discriminator

text_en: Adult male. Stabbing injury to chest. RR 38, HR 140, SBP 78. Unresponsive to voice. Trachea deviated.
Woman 32 weeks pregnant. Generalised tonic-clonic seizure ongoing. BP 190/130. Unresponsive.
Child 5 years. Near drowning. Pulled from water unresponsive. No spontaneous breathing. No pulse. Family confirms child was swimming 5 minutes ago. Rescuer performing mouth-to-mouth.
note: Drowning cardiac arrest — recent submersion confirmed, CPR in progress

text_en: Adult male. Crush injury to abdomen from road accident. HR 158, SBP 74, RR 34, AVPU = P. Abdomen rigid.
Infant 4 months. Not breathing, cyanotic. Brought in by mother who found baby unresponsive in cot 2 minutes ago. No pulse. CPR not yet started.
note: Infant cardiac arrest — found 2 minutes ago, resuscitation indicated

text_en: Adult female. Severe anaphylaxis after bee sting. Stridor, SpO2 82%, HR 142, SBP 76. Unresponsive.
note: Anaphylactic shock — SpO2 < 85 and SBP < 80 with HR > 130

text_en: Child 7 years. Active seizure ongoing 9 minutes. Postictal, unresponsive. No fever. Known epilepsy.
Adult male. Electric shock at work site. Found collapsed 2 minutes ago. No breathing. No pulse. Bystanders started CPR immediately. Resuscitation in progress — not confirmed dead.
note: Electrocution cardiac arrest — found 2 minutes ago, CPR in progress, resuscitation indicated

text_en: Adult female. Post-partum haemorrhage. Delivered 1 hour ago. Soaked 6 pads. HR 152, SBP 72. AVPU = U, unresponsive. Haemorrhagic shock.
note: Post-partum haemorrhage with haemorrhagic shock — SBP < 80 with HR > 130 and AVPU = U

text_en: Woman 36 weeks pregnant. Fitting. No prior history. BP 210/140. Unresponsive between contractions.
Adult male. Gunshot wound to neck. Uncontrolled arterial bleeding. HR 162, SBP 68, RR 36, AVPU = P.
Child 18 months. Febrile seizure lasting 7 minutes. Still seizing on arrival. Unresponsive. Temp 40.3°C.

Adult male. Found hanging, cut down 3 minutes ago. Cardiac arrest from hanging — resuscitation indicated and in progress. No breathing, no pulse currently. Bystanders performing CPR. Not yet confirmed dead.
note: Hanging cardiac arrest — actively being resuscitated, confirmed less than 5 minutes down

text_en: Adult female. Snake bite 30 minutes ago. SpO2 78%, HR 136, SBP 78, RR 32, AVPU = V.
Adult male. Central chest pain 45 min. Radiates to left arm. Sweating. HR 112, RR 22.
Female. Sudden weakness right arm and face droop 1 hour ago. Responds to voice only. HR 96, RR 20.
note: Acute stroke — very urgent discriminator, AVPU=V

text_en: Child 18 months. RR 52, HR 180, temperature 39.9°C, SpO2 91%. Grunting. Alert.
Adult. Diabetic. Blood glucose 1.8 mmol/L. Confused, responds to voice. HR 108, RR 18.
Adult male. Sudden severe headache, worst of life, onset instantaneous. Vomiting. Fully alert. HR 88, RR 18, BP 168/100. Suspected subarachnoid haemorrhage requiring urgent CT.
note: Suspected SAH — very urgent discriminator, time-critical neurological emergency

text_en: Child 3 years. RR 58, SpO2 88%, HR 168, temp 40°C. Intercostal recession. Alert but distressed.
Adult female. Sudden left arm weakness and slurred speech 2 hours ago. Alert, AVPU = V. HR 92, BP 178/104.
Adult male. Fever 39.8°C, HR 118, RR 26, BP 88/56. Confusion. Suspected source: urinary infection.
Adult female. Central chest tightness 1 hour. Jaw pain. Nausea. Diaphoretic. HR 108, BP 142/88. Alert.
note: Suspected ACS in female — atypical presentation

text_en: Adult male. Blood glucose 1.2 mmol/L. Drowsy, responds only to voice. Diabetic on insulin. HR 102.
note: Severe hypoglycaemia — glucose < 3 and AVPU = V

text_en: Adult male. Severe asthma attack. SpO2 88%, RR 34, HR 128. Speaks in single words. Alert.
Adult male. Fever 39.4°C, HR 114, RR 24, BP 92/58. Confused and agitated, responds to voice only. Suspected source lower respiratory tract. Severe sepsis.
note: Severe sepsis — AVPU = V, very urgent discriminator

text_en: Child 4 years. SpO2 87%, RR 48, HR 152, temp 39.2°C. Nasal flaring, subcostal recession. Alert.
Adult male. Left-sided chest pain with exertion, relieved at rest. Diaphoresis. HR 116, BP 136/82. Alert.
Adult female. Sudden severe abdominal pain. 20 weeks pregnant. HR 118, BP 88/60. Fully alert and talking, AVPU = A. Guarding on palpation. Suspected ectopic rupture.
note: Suspected ectopic rupture — very urgent, alert patient with haemodynamic instability

text_en: Adult male. Temp 40.2°C, HR 122, RR 28, BP 86/54. Responds to voice. Neck stiffness. Photophobia.
note: Suspected meningitis with sepsis — AVPU = V

text_en: Adult male. Crushing central chest pain radiating to back. HR 104, BP 148/92. Diaphoresis. Alert.
note: Suspected aortic dissection — very urgent discriminator

text_en: Child 6 years. SpO2 89%, RR 44, HR 144, temp 38.8°C. Grunting on expiration. Alert but lethargic.
Adult male. Blood glucose 1.6 mmol/L. Sweating, confused, responds to voice. HR 108, RR 20.
note: Severe hypoglycaemia — glucose < 3 and AVPU = V

text_en: Adult female. Right arm weakness and facial droop on waking 3 hours ago. Acute ischaemic stroke, time-critical for thrombolysis. Alert, AVPU = A. BP 172/98.
note: Acute stroke — within thrombolysis window, very urgent discriminator

text_en: Adult male. Chest pain for 2 hours, dull and non-radiating. Fully alert and talking. HR 88, RR 16, SBP 138. No diaphoresis, no radiation to arm or jaw. Pain 4/10.
note: Chest pain with stable vitals — borderline orange/yellow

text_en: Woman. Vomiting blood twice. Conscious and alert. HR 98, RR 18, SBP 105.
Child 4 years. Fever 38.9°C for 2 days. Alert, crying. RR 28, HR 118.

Adult. Head injury from motorbike accident. Brief loss of consciousness lasting under 1 minute, now fully alert and oriented. GCS 14, HR 88, RR 16, SBP 122. No vomiting, no neurological deficit. Stable for urgent assessment.
note: Head injury GCS 14 — urgent discriminator, stable vitals

text_en: Adult male. Moderate chest pain for 3 hours. Alert. HR 94, RR 18, SBP 132. Pain is 6/10.
note: Chest pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator

text_en: Child 8 years. Fever 39.2°C for 3 days. Alert and playful. RR 24, HR 108. Eating reduced.

Adult female. Moderate lower abdominal pain since yesterday, worsening. Alert. HR 88, RR 18, SBP 118. Tender on palpation with guarding. Requires urgent assessment to exclude appendicitis or pelvic pathology.
note: Moderate abdominal pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator

text_en: Adult male. Fell from bicycle. Head hit ground. Brief confusion now alert. GCS 14. HR 82, RR 16.
Woman. Vomiting blood once. Stable. Alert. HR 96, RR 18, SBP 110. Known peptic ulcer.
Child 6 years. Fever 38.8°C. Alert, eating and drinking. RR 26, HR 112. Mild cough. No seizures.
Adult male. Severe acute back pain after lifting, pain 8/10. Completely unable to walk or stand. Alert. HR 84, RR 16, SBP 126.
note: Moderate pain with stable vitals — urgent discriminator

text_en: Adult male. Fell from standing height. Hit head. Alert. GCS 14. Mild headache. HR 78, RR 16.
Child 3 years. Fever 39.5°C for 1 day. Alert, crying. RR 30 (well below paediatric threshold), HR 122. No rash, no neck stiffness. No respiratory distress.
note: Febrile child, alert — RR 30 below urgent threshold, urgent discriminator

text_en: Adult female. Single episode of vomiting blood. Conscious and talking. HR 102, SBP 108, RR 20.
Adult male. Motorcycle accident. Helmet worn. Alert. GCS 14. Confused for 5 minutes. HR 88, RR 18.
Child 10 years. Fever 39.1°C. Alert and talking. RR 22, HR 106. Complaining of sore throat and ear pain.
Adult female. Right-sided chest pain after cough, pleuritic in nature — worse on inspiration, no radiation, no diaphoresis. Alert. HR 86, RR 22, SBP 124.
note: Pleuritic chest pain — not cardiac, stable vitals, urgent discriminator

text_en: Adult male. Vomited dark blood once. Alert. HR 98, SBP 112, RR 18. Feels weak but stable.
Child 2 years. Fever 38.7°C for 2 days. Alert, irritable. RR 28, HR 116. Drinking well. No seizures.
Adult female. Moderate abdominal pain, 8 weeks pregnant. Alert. HR 92, SBP 118, RR 18. No bleeding.
note: Moderate pain in pregnancy with stable vitals — urgent discriminator

text_en: Minor laceration to left forearm. Patient alert and oriented. HR 80, RR 16, SBP 120.
note: Minor wound — no discriminators, normal TEWS

text_en: Sore throat 3 days. Low-grade fever 37.8°C. Fully alert. Eating and drinking normally.
Routine antenatal visit. 24 weeks pregnant. No complaints. All vitals normal.
Patient with cough for 6 weeks. No fever, no haemoptysis, no weight loss, no night sweats. Fully alert. Normal vitals.
note: Chronic cough — no red flags for TB, not urgent

text_en: Adult male. Mild headache for 2 days. Alert. HR 72, RR 14, SBP 118. No fever, no vomiting, no neurological signs.
Adult female. Mild ankle sprain from walking on uneven ground. Alert. Able to weight bear. Normal vitals.
Child 5 years. Runny nose and mild cough for 4 days. Alert and playful. No fever. Eating well.
Adult male. Small superficial burn on right hand from cooking. Alert. Less than 1% body surface area. Normal vitals.
Adult female. Routine follow-up for hypertension medication refill. BP 136/84, well-controlled. Alert. No symptoms. Routine visit only.
note: Routine medication review — well-controlled hypertension, no urgency

text_en: Child 9 years. Mild earache for 2 days. Alert and playing. No fever. HR 82, RR 18. Eating normally.
Adult male. Mild diarrhoea for 1 day. No blood in stool. Alert. Normal vitals. Drinking fluids.
Adult female. Routine postnatal visit at 6 weeks. Fully alert. No complaints. Normal vitals. Breastfeeding well.
Adult male. Mild lower back pain for 3 days. Alert. Normal vitals. No neurological symptoms. Able to walk.
note: Non-specific low back pain — no discriminators

text_en: Child 7 years. Mild conjunctivitis both eyes. Alert. No fever. No pain. Eating and drinking normally.
Adult male. Mild insect bite on left arm. Local redness and swelling. Alert. Normal vitals. No systemic symptoms.
Adult female. Routine immunisation visit. Alert and well. Normal vitals.
Adult male. Mild toothache for 2 days. Alert. Normal vitals. No facial swelling. No fever.
Child 4 years. Mild rash on trunk, no fever, alert and playing. Chickenpox suspected. Eating normally.
Adult female. Mild nausea without vomiting for 1 day. First trimester pregnancy. No bleeding, no pain, no fever. Alert. Normal vitals. Normal first trimester.
note: Morning sickness — no complications, no discriminators

text_en: Adult male. Mild skin rash on forearm. Alert. No fever. No systemic symptoms. Normal vitals. Contact with plant suspected.
Patient declared deceased on arrival. No pulse, no breathing, fixed dilated pupils. Rigor mortis present.
Elderly patient. End-stage cancer. Family requests comfort care only. Breathing agonal, unresponsive. Cold extremities, mottled skin. Expected imminent death.
note: Expectant — end-stage cancer, actively dying, comfort care per family

text_en: Burns patient. 95% total body surface area full thickness burns. No survivable prognosis.
Mass casualty. Patient with penetrating head injury, fixed dilated pupils, no spontaneous breathing.
Adult male. Found at home. No breathing, no pulse. Body cold to touch. Rigor mortis in all limbs.
note: Confirmed death — all BLUE criteria met

text_en: Adult female. End-stage HIV with wasting. Agonal breathing only. Unresponsive. Cold body, mottled skin. Death expected within minutes. Family declines resuscitation.
note: Expectant — end-stage HIV, actively dying, DNR per family

text_en: Adult male. Brought in after road accident. Decapitation injury. No vital signs. Confirmed unsurvivable injuries.
note: Confirmed death from unsurvivable traumatic injury

text_en: Elderly female. Advanced dementia, end of life. No breathing, no pulse. Found by carer. Body cold. Fixed pupils.
note: Confirmed death in advanced dementia patient

text_en: Adult male. Drowning. Submerged for estimated 45 minutes. No vital signs. Fixed dilated pupils. Cold body.
Adult female. Terminal cancer. Comfort care only agreed. Agonal breathing. Unresponsive. HR 22, RR 2.
note: Expectant — terminal cancer, comfort care per advance directive

text_en: Child 2 years. Found submerged in bucket. No breathing, no pulse. Fixed dilated pupils. Estimated 30 minutes submersion.
note: Paediatric drowning — prolonged submersion, confirmed death

text_en: Adult male. Burns greater than 90% total body surface area, full thickness. No survivable prognosis even with full resuscitation in any setting. Unresponsive.
note: Expectant — unsurvivable burns, no resuscitation indicated in any setting

text_en: Adult female. Mass casualty. Traumatic brain injury with brain matter visible. Fixed pupils. No spontaneous breathing.
note: Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable traumatic brain injury

text_en: Elderly male. Found in bed. No breathing, no pulse. Cold body. Rigor mortis. Family reports ill for weeks.
note: Confirmed death at home — all BLUE criteria met

text_en: Adult male. End-stage renal failure. Dialysis refused. Agonal breathing. Unresponsive. HR 18, RR 3.
Adult male. Explosion injury. Bilateral limb amputations, evisceration. No vital signs.
note: Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable blast injuries

text_en: Elderly female. Advanced heart failure. DNR agreed. Found unresponsive by family. No breathing, no pulse.
note: Confirmed death with existing DNR order

text_en: Adult male. Lightning strike. No vital signs on arrival. Fixed dilated pupils. Cold body.
Adult female. Terminal liver failure. Jaundiced, comatose. HR 14, RR 2. Family present, no resuscitation.
Adult male. Mass casualty. Penetrating abdominal trauma with full evisceration. No vital signs.
note: Expectant in mass casualty — unsurvivable without unavailable surgery
